text.title: Safe Refactoring Workflow
text.summary: The one-pattern-per-commit loop, quality gates, and agent-specific gotchas.

rule.statement: Apply exactly one pattern per commit: plan → apply diff → run full test suite → commit if green, revert if red.
rule.rationale: Atomic commits mean a single git-bisect step can locate any regression introduced by refactoring.

rule.statement: Require at least 80% line coverage on touched code before starting; if coverage is below that threshold, write characterization tests first.
rule.rationale: Without a safety net, agents silently change behavior while claiming preservation.

rule.statement: Never mix refactoring commits with feature changes; a commit either changes behavior or changes structure, never both.
rule.rationale: Mixed commits defeat bisect and make code review impossible for reviewers trying to verify behavior preservation.

section.title: Subagent Architecture
section.p: Drive refactoring as a TDD-style loop with specialized subagents.

list.item: coverage-checker: reports per-line coverage on the target file/range; gates the loop entry.
refactor-planner (Sonnet/Opus): reads code, picks ONE pattern from the catalog, explains why, generates unified diff + test command.
refactor-applier: applies the diff via Edit tool, runs tests, commits if green, reverts and reports if red.
rename-propagator: handles cross-file renames via AST tools; non-LLM where possible.
simplify (built-in skill): periodic "step back, simplify what we just wrote" pass to catch over-abstraction.

section.reference: Bash loop implementing the plan-apply-test-commit cycle.

antipattern.description: Big-bang refactoring: changing many patterns in one commit.
antipattern.reason: Impossible to bisect; code review becomes meaningless.
Agents claim behavior preservation while introducing subtle off-by-ones (rounding, datetime, encoding).

antipattern.description: Over-abstraction: introducing a Strategy class for a 3-branch conditional.
antipattern.reason: Abstraction debt exceeds the original debt; force a 4-branch minimum rule in the prompt.

rule.statement: Demand verbatim test coverage on every touched line before claiming behavior equivalence.
rule.rationale: Agents freely convert Decimal to float, reorder short-circuit evaluation, and change exception types while calling the result equivalent.

rule.statement: Lock the agent to the closed catalog; reject any prompt that says "improve" without naming a specific pattern.
rule.rationale: Open-ended "improve" prompts produce taste-driven churn, not structured refactoring.

rule.statement: Constrain commit messages to 50-char title + at most 3-line body.
rule.rationale: Agents write verbose commit essays; clean history requires a hard constraint.
